summary: Matched a large set of previously stubbed or partially matching functions in Kirby's copied neutral-special implementation file, especially Sheik needle and Mewtwo shadow-ball copy behavior. The PR added concrete C for item spawning, charge loops, release/recoil, IASA cancel/fire handling, SFX/effect thresholds, and cleanup callbacks, raising the decomp.dev report by 18 new matches and +6208 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Only changed file; implemented matches and cleanup for Kirby copied neutral-special behavior.

what_changed: Added include for `it/items/itseakneedlethrown.h` and externs for `ftKb_Init_803B7570`, `ftKb_Init_803B757C`, `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, and later `ftKb_Init_803CB828` to support item/effect/SFX table behavior.
Implemented `ftKb_SpecialNSk_8010603C`, consuming `fp->fv.kb.xB4` needle count and spawning thrown needles with `it_802AFD8C`/`it_802AFEA8` at ground/air y-offsets.
Added inline `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs` and reused it where multiple SpecialN start functions set `death2_cb` and `take_dmg_cb` to Kirby cleanup handlers.
Matched small Sheik-copy animation loops by changing temporary variable and switch-case structure around `ft_PlaySFX`, and changed a pointer clear from `0` to `NULL`.

Implemented `fn_80106DB0`, spawning individual Sheik needles with randomized y-offsets from `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, effect `0x503`, and SFX `0x41F3C`; it uses a local `perm_randi` wrapper around `HSD_Randi` noted as permuter-generated.

Implemented Mewtwo-copy release logic in `ftKb_SpecialNMt_801071FC`, including hand-position calculation via `lb_8000B1CC`, launch angle selection based on facing direction, `it_802C53F0`, recoil setup, saved/restored `item_gobj`, and release SFX.
Added `ftKb_SpecialNMt_SetRecoil` to apply ground or air horizontal momentum from Mewtwo charge data depending on motion/air-ground state.
Implemented `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107410`, tracking charge ratio crossing 0, 0.25, 0.5, and 0.75 thresholds and playing entries from `ftKb_Init_803CB828`.

Reworked Mewtwo ground/air SpecialN start functions `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107568` and `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107638` to match directly, reset command variables and timers, set callbacks via helper, and initialize transition timing from `specialn_mt_frames_to_transition`.

Implemented Mewtwo ground and air start/loop animation functions, creating held shadow-ball items with `it_802C5000`, advancing charge with `specialn_mt_loops_to_full_charge`, switching to full-charge loop states, and spawning full-charge effects with `efSync_Spawn(0x1B, ...)`.

Implemented ground and air Mewtwo loop/full-loop IASA functions, handling A/B fire inputs, a high-bit cancel input mask `0x80000000`, item cleanup via `it_802C573C`, effect cleanup via `efLib_DestroyAll`, and `ftKirbyDmgInline` updates.
Completed `fn_801090D4` for another Kirby copy path in the same file, using `cmd_vars[0]` to spawn/release an item with `it_802C1590`/`it_802C16F8`, SFX, and callback setup.
Bot report recorded 18 new matches in `main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNZd` and one `.sdata2` improvement from 37.50% to 79.07%.
smart_moves: Factored repeated death/damage callback setup into `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`, reducing duplicate code while preserving matching at call sites.

Introduced small inline helpers for repeated behavior (`ftKb_SpecialNMt_SetRecoil`, `ftKb_SpecialNMt_CreateHeldShadow`) where that helped express repeated ground/air Mewtwo-copy logic without preventing matches.

Used exact stack-shape tactics visible in the diff, including `PAD_STACK`, explicit local `Vec3` temporaries, dummy `f64` pads, and even `fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`/`da = da = fp->dat_attrs` patterns where needed for codegen.
Preserved and restored `fp->item_gobj` around Mewtwo shadow-ball release, a behavior-sensitive detail that likely avoided side effects while matching.

Kept ground and air loop implementations very similar but separate, using distinct full-charge motion IDs and vector constants (`ftKb_Init_803B7570` vs `ftKb_Init_803B757C`) rather than over-abstracting away codegen differences.

Acknowledged a matching-only artifact with the comment `permuter-generated helper, almost certainly fake` for `perm_randi`, making the decompromise explicit instead of presenting it as a known source-level symbol.
Replaced integer zero pointer writes with `NULL` in touched pointer fields, improving type clarity while still matching.
decomp_lessons: For fighter action functions, matching may require deliberate local variable lifetime and stack layout control; `PAD_STACK`, extra local blocks, and duplicate `GET_FIGHTER` calls can be legitimate tools.

Do not prematurely consolidate mirrored ground/air action logic: Mewtwo-copy ground and air paths share structure but differ in motion IDs, vectors, velocity fields, freefall handling, and transition targets.

Kirby copy-ability code often coordinates fighter state, item ownership, effects, sound, callbacks, and damage-inline updates in the same action function; all of those side effects matter for faithful reconstruction.
Charge-loop code should preserve threshold-crossing semantics, not just final charge value; `ftKb_SpecialNMt_80107410` only plays SFX when charge ratio crosses configured thresholds.

When an item pointer is cleared or destroyed, also inspect associated effect pointers and callback cleanup; this PR repeatedly clears `fv.kb.x98`, destroys effects, and resets `fv.kb.xA0` in cancel paths.
Externing data constants and tables can unlock `.sdata2`/rodata progress along with code matches, as shown by the bot-reported `.sdata2` improvement.
Document permuter artifacts when a helper or wrapper is probably not source-real, so future cleanup can revisit it without losing the matching rationale.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied neutral-special symbols use the `ftKb_SpecialN*` and `ftKb_*SpecialN*` prefixes, with copy-source abbreviations such as `Sk` for Sheik, `Mt` for Mewtwo, `Ic`, `Pp`, and `Ys` appearing in the file.
Unidentified functions retained address-style names such as `fn_80106DB0` and `fn_801090D4` when semantic names were not established.

Dat attribute fields use descriptive `specialn_<copy>_<property>` names, for example `specialn_sk_graphic_y_offset_ground`, `specialn_mt_charge_time`, `specialn_mt_loops_to_full_charge`, and `specialn_pp_air_vertical_momentum`.
Motion-state names for Kirby Mewtwo copy follow `ftKb_MS_MtSpecialN...` and `ftKb_MS_MtSpecialAirN...` ground/air variants.
Callback helper naming in this PR used lower-case internal helper style for new static inline code: `ftKb_SpecialN_set_cbs`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `PAD_STACK` with sizes including 4, 8, 16, 24, and `0x10` to force stack layout in newly matched functions.
Used dummy locals such as `f64 _pad1`, `f64 _pad2`, and `u8 _[8]` where needed to match generated stack allocation.
Manipulated temporary declarations and scopes, such as nested blocks around `Vec3 pos` handling and switch `case 0` blocks with a fresh `Fighter* fp`, to improve register allocation/codegen.

Kept non-idiomatic assignments like `Fighter* fp = fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj);` and `ftKb_DatAttrs* da = da = fp->dat_attrs;` in some matched functions, indicating compiler-codegen constraints outweighed style.
Used ternary assignment for timers in start-action setup where it matched better than a separate `timer` local.
Added a wrapper `perm_randi(int n) { return HSD_Randi(n); }` specifically to satisfy permuter/matching output, with a comment warning it is probably fake.
Used explicit casts and float comparisons in charge and full-charge checks, e.g. `(f32) fp->fv.kb.x9C == da->specialn_mt_charge_time` and `(s32) da->specialn_mt_loops_to_full_charge`.

review_feedback.feedback: GALE01 report showed matched code increased to 59.88% (+0.16%, +6208 bytes), with 18 new matches in `ftKb_SpecialNZd` and one `.sdata2` improvement.
review_feedback.action_taken: PR was merged without visible human review comments in the provided slice.

review_feedback.feedback: No human review comments or PR body text were present in the context.
review_feedback.action_taken: Reviewer intent beyond the bot match report is not evidenced.

follow_up_queries: Check whether `perm_randi` can later be removed or replaced with a source-real helper while preserving `fn_80106DB0`.
Investigate semantic names for `fn_80106DB0` and `fn_801090D4` once adjacent Kirby copy-ability behavior is better understood.
Look for duplicated held-item cleanup patterns in other Kirby copied SpecialN functions that could inform safe helper extraction without breaking matches.
Confirm data-symbol names and types for `ftKb_Init_803B7570`, `ftKb_Init_803B757C`, `ftKb_Init_803CB770`, and `ftKb_Init_803CB828` as `.sdata2`/rodata work continues.
